title: Solayer Launches Visa Card for USDC Payments in the US
summary: Solayer has introduced a Visa-compatible card that allows users to spend USDC balances for online, in-store, and contactless transactions. The card also enables ATM withdrawals in supported regions, making stablecoin spending more accessible.
body:
Solayer, a blockchain infrastructure provider, has launched a Visa-compatible card that lets users spend their USDC (USD Coin) balances seamlessly. The card supports online, in-store, and contactless transactions, making it easier to use stablecoins in everyday purchases.

The new card also allows users to withdraw cash from ATMs in supported regions, further integrating USDC into traditional financial systems. Solayer aims to bridge the gap between cryptocurrency and traditional payment methods, offering a user-friendly way to spend digital assets.

This development is significant for everyday users who hold USDC, as it provides a practical way to use their stablecoin holdings without converting them to fiat currency. The card simplifies the process of spending USDC, making it as easy as using a traditional debit or credit card.

For those interested in using the card, it's important to check the list of supported regions for ATM withdrawals and ensure their USDC balances are held in a compatible wallet. Keep an eye on Solayer's announcements for any updates on additional features or supported cryptocurrencies.
twitterThread: Solayer's new Visa card lets you spend USDC anywhere Visa is accepted—no conversion needed. 💳
The card works for online, in-store, and contactless payments, plus ATM withdrawals in supported regions. USDC just got more practical.

Want to use your USDC for everyday spending? Check if your region supports ATM withdrawals. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-05-16-solayer-launches-visa-card-for-usdc-payments-in-the-us
standaloneTweet: Solayer's new Visa card lets you spend USDC like a regular debit card—online, in-store, and even at ATMs in supported regions. 💳
